settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage tracked keywords and the monitoring scheduler. Defaults: every 6 hours, single keyword.

help.title: How this works
help.subtitle: Plain English: what you're looking at, how I classify, why some end up unclear.

intro.title: What you're looking at
intro.body: I regularly check Google Netherlands for the query 'starcasino' and show you who's in the top results. The dashboard tells you: which site IS the brand, who's honestly promoting it for a commission, and who's exploiting the brand's name to redirect users to a different casino.

categories.title: Four kinds of sites
categories.affiliate: An honest partner. Runs reviews or 'best casinos' lists, but when a user wants to sign up they're sent to StarCasino. Earns a cut on each referred customer.
categories.thief: The most threatening category. Ranks on the 'starcasino' query — uses the brand as bait — but the 'Play' button leads to a different casino (Jack's, TonyBet, Holland Casino). Intercepts a customer who came looking for the brand.
categories.unclear: Not enough signal to classify confidently. Typically Wikipedia, Trustpilot, Google Play Store — these pages simply have no monetised buttons.

signals.title: What I look at
signals.intro: The algorithm combines a simple rule set with an LLM's opinion (Claude). Two groups of signals:
signals.serpBody: Visible right away, without even opening the site.

serpItems.0: Is the page on the StarCasino domain itself
serpItems.1: Is the page on a known competitor's domain (jacks.nl, tonybet.nl…)
serpItems.2: Does the domain contain the word 'starcasino' (typosquat — e.g. starcasino-nl.com)
serpItems.3: Does the page title look like a review or ranking ('review', 'top 10', 'beste', 'alternatieven')

signals.pageBody: More accurate, but not always available — many casino sites block bots.

pageItems.0: Where the main 'Play' button leads — to StarCasino, or to a competitor
pageItems.2: How often the brand name appears in the page body
pageItems.3: Whether there's a visible affiliate disclosure (EU/FTC requirement)

signals.fusion: All signals add up into a per-category score. The category with the highest score wins. If no category scores high enough, the verdict is 'Unclear'. The LLM is used as a second opinion on borderline cases.

unclear.title: Why some end up 'Unclear'
unclear.body1: The most accurate way to tell an 'affiliate of the brand' from a 'brand thief' is to click the 'Play' button and see where the redirect chain physically ends. Ends at starcasino.nl with an affiliate tag → affiliate. Ends at a different casino → thief.
unclear.body2: But many casino sites are bot-protected (Cloudflare, DataDome). When the system tries to open the page, it gets 'verify you're not a bot' instead of real content. So the 'Play' button is sometimes simply invisible to us.
unclear.calloutBody: The prototype has a floor on 'Unclear' — you can't get below it without actively clicking buttons. A production system would add a separate browser that physically clicks the button, follows the redirects, and records where it landed. That's beyond the prototype's scope.

monitoring.title: How regular checks work
monitoring.body: By default the system checks the SERP every 4 hours (180 runs/month, within SerpAPI's free tier of 250/month). In Settings you can change the interval, fire a one-off check, or stop the auto-checks. Every run is saved — the History chart shows how the mix shifts over time.

stack.title: Under the hood
stack.body: Node.js + TypeScript on the backend. Playwright for scraping (drives a real Chromium browser). SerpAPI for Google results. SQLite for storage. Claude Opus 4.7 via OpenRouter as a second opinion. React + Tailwind on the frontend. Packaged in Docker, hosted on a Contabo VPS, fronted by Caddy with a Let's Encrypt certificate.

keyword.title: Tracked keywords
keyword.hint: Each keyword is monitored on every scheduler tick. Switch which one the dashboard shows from the header.
